summary: Professional luxury e-commerce mobile app for a fashion/lifestyle brand selling Clothing (men, women, kids), Perfumes, and Jewelry. Features a premium UI with home banners, product listings, cart, wishlist, secure multi-payment checkout, user auth, order tracking, admin panel, push notifications, and search/filter. Targets Android and iOS.
architectural_notes: Premium, elegant luxury brand style targeting fashion and lifestyle products
Multi-platform target: Android and iOS (React Native or Flutter recommended)
Auth via phone number and email; secure payment integration required

existing_features.title: Apply a premium luxury visual theme across the entire app: deep charcoal or soft black background, gold/champagne accent colors, cream/ivory text, elegant serif headings paired with clean sans-serif body text, generous whitespace, and subtle shadow/glow effects. The aesthetic must feel high-end and fashion-forward, suitable for jewelry, perfumes, and designer clothing.

Implement a Home screen that displays a full-width hero banner carousel (auto-scrolling with manual swipe support) for promotions and new arrivals, followed by a horizontal category quick-access row (Clothes, Perfumes, Jewelry), a 'Featured Products' horizontal scroll section, and a 'New Arrivals' grid section.

Implement a Product Catalog / Listing page that shows products in a 2-column grid. Each product card must display: product image, name, price, discounted price (with percentage off badge if applicable), and an 'Add to Wishlist' heart icon. Support filtering by category (Clothes → sub-categories: Men, Women, Kids; Perfumes; Jewelry), price range, and sort by popularity/price/newest.

Build a Product Detail page showing: large product image gallery (swipeable), product name, brand, price with original/discounted price, description, size selector (for Clothing), color/variant selector, quantity picker, star ratings with aggregate score, paginated user reviews (reviewer name, rating, comment, date), and two CTAs — 'Add to Cart' and 'Add to Wishlist'.

Implement Cart and Wishlist functionality. The Cart page must list added items with image, name, variant, quantity controls (+/-), and remove option, plus an order summary with subtotal, discount, and total. The Wishlist page lists saved products with the ability to move items to cart or remove them.

Build a Checkout flow with: shipping address entry, order summary review, and a payment selection step supporting UPI, Credit/Debit Card (with card number/expiry/CVV fields), Wallet, and Cash on Delivery options. On confirmation, create the order in the backend with status 'Pending' and display an order confirmation screen with order ID.

Implement user authentication with Internet Identity (the platform's supported auth mechanism) — map it to a user profile that stores display name, email address, and phone number. Provide a Profile page where users can view and update their stored name, email, and phone number.

Implement an Order History page showing all past orders for the logged-in user, with order ID, date, item thumbnails, total, and current status. Implement an Order Detail / Tracking page showing a visual status timeline (Placed → Confirmed → Shipped → Out for Delivery → Delivered) with timestamps for each status update.

Build an Admin Panel accessible only to a designated admin principal. The admin panel must support: Add Product (name, description, category, sub-category, images via URL, price, discount, stock, variants), Edit Product, Delete Product, view all orders, and update order status (Confirmed / Shipped / Out for Delivery / Delivered).

Implement a global Search bar in the navigation header. Searching by keyword should query product names and descriptions and display results in a filtered product grid. Results must be filterable by the same price/category/popularity filters as the main listing page.

Generate and use custom brand imagery: a luxury hero banner for the home carousel, a category cover image for Clothes, one for Perfumes, one for Jewelry, and a brand logo mark. Render all generated images from frontend/public/assets/generated.

Ensure every frontend source file is complete and runnable: all React components, pages, hooks, and utilities contain full implementations with no TODO placeholders, type errors, or missing imports. The app must compile with `tsc --noEmit` passing cleanly.

Revert the entire application (both frontend and backend) to its Version 1 state, undoing all changes introduced in Version 2. Restore all frontend source files, backend Motoko logic, styles, configuration files, and assets to exactly the state they were in at Version 1.

Rebuild and redeploy the application in its current Version 1 state — all frontend source files, backend Motoko logic, styles, configuration files, and assets — exactly as they stand, with no modifications. This is a clean production push of the existing Version 1 codebase.

new_feature_requests.summary: Home screen with banners and featured products
Product listing with images, price, discount, size/color options
Product detail page with description, reviews and ratings
Add to cart and wishlist functionality
Secure checkout with UPI, Credit/Debit Card, Wallet, Cash on Delivery
User login/signup with phone number and email
Admin panel to add, edit and manage products
Push notifications for offers and new arrivals
Search and filter by price, category, popularity
Responsive design for Android and iOS

Define the full Motoko backend data model and API. Required entities: Product (id, name, description, category, subCategory, imageUrls, price, discountPercent, stock, variants:[{size, color, quantity}], createdAt), User (principal, name, email, phone), Cart (userId, items:[{productId, variant, quantity}]), Wishlist (userId, productIds), Order (id, userId, items, shippingAddress, paymentMethod, totalAmount, status, statusHistory:[{status, timestamp}]), Review (id, productId, userId, rating, comment, date). Expose query and update functions for all CRUD operations needed by the frontend.

Ensure the Motoko backend (backend/main.mo) is complete and runnable: all types (Product, User, Cart, Wishlist, Order, Review) are fully defined, all CRUD query and update endpoints are implemented with no stubs or placeholders, role-based admin access control is enforced, and the file compiles without errors.

Ensure the full project structure is present and well-organized: backend/main.mo, frontend/index.html, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/App.tsx, all page files under frontend/src/pages/, all component files under frontend/src/components/, all hook files under frontend/src/hooks/, all utility files under frontend/src/utils/, frontend/index.css, frontend/src/index.css, frontend/tailwind.config.js, and any configuration files (dfx.json, package.json, tsconfig.json) must exist and be correctly configured.

Add a comprehensive README.md at the project root with step-by-step setup and run instructions covering: prerequisites (Node.js, DFX SDK versions), installation (`npm install`), local replica startup (`dfx start`), canister deployment (`dfx deploy`), accessing the frontend URL, configuring the admin principal, and a brief description of all major features.
